Child Vaccination Crisis: A Global Perspective

Author

Tushar Jagadish

Published

April 15, 2026

Introduction

“Every child deserves a healthy start in life.” — UNICEF

Rotavirus is the leading cause of severe diarrhoeal disease in children under five globally. Despite the availability of safe and effective vaccines, millions of children remain unprotected — particularly in low-income countries.

This report explores:

  • The global distribution of rotavirus vaccine coverage
  • How coverage changed over time (2018–2022)
  • The relationship between vaccine access and economic development indicators

Setup

Code 
import subprocess
subprocess.run(["pip", "install", "plotnine", "geopandas", "mapclassify"], 
               capture_output=True)

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
from plotnine import *
import os

# Download world shapefile
subprocess.run([
    "wget", "-q", "-O", "ne_110m.zip",
    "https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip"
])
subprocess.run(["unzip", "-q", "-o", "ne_110m.zip"])

# Load data
indicator = pd.read_csv("unicef_indicator_1.csv")
metadata  = pd.read_csv("unicef_metadata.csv")

# --- Transformations ---
latest = (indicator
    .groupby(["country", "alpha_3_code"], as_index=False)
    .agg(obs_value=("obs_value", "last"))
)

meta_latest = (metadata
    .sort_values("year")
    .groupby("country", as_index=False)
    .last()
)

merged = latest.merge(meta_latest, on="country", how="left")

time_series = (indicator
    .groupby("time_period", as_index=False)["obs_value"].mean()
    .rename(columns={"obs_value": "avg_coverage"})
)

top20 = latest.nlargest(20, "obs_value")

print("✅ Setup complete")
✅ Setup complete

World Map: Vaccine Coverage by Country

The map below reveals stark geographic inequalities in rotavirus immunisation. Countries in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia show the lowest coverage rates, while Europe and the Americas perform considerably better.

Code 
world = gpd.read_file("ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp")
world = world.merge(latest, left_on="ADM0_A3", right_on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

map_plot = (
    ggplot(world)
    + aes(fill="obs_value")
    + geom_map(color="white", size=0.1)
    + scale_fill_gradient(low="#fee8c8", high="#b30000",
                          na_value="#d3d3d3", name="Coverage (%)")
    + labs(title="Global Rotavirus Vaccine Coverage",
           subtitle="Grey = no data available",
           caption="Source: UNICEF")
    + theme_void()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(size=14, face="bold"),
            plot_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
            legend_position="bottom")
)
map_plot.draw()

Bar Chart: Top 20 Countries

The following countries have achieved near-universal coverage, demonstrating that high immunisation rates are achievable with strong health infrastructure and political commitment.

Code 
bar_plot = (
    ggplot(top20, aes(x="reorder(country, obs_value)", y="obs_value", fill="obs_value"))
    + geom_col()
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_gradient(low="#fdbb84", high="#b30000", name="Coverage (%)")
    + labs(title="Top 20 Countries — Rotavirus Vaccine Coverage",
           x="Country", y="Coverage (%)", caption="Source: UNICEF")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
            plot_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
            legend_position="none")
)
bar_plot.draw()

Scatterplot: Coverage vs GDP per Capita

The scatterplot below shows a positive relationship between a country’s GDP per capita and its rotavirus vaccine coverage rate.

  • Wealthier countries tend to have better coverage
  • Colour encodes life expectancy — a strong co-indicator of development
  • The regression line confirms the positive trend, though with notable outliers
Code 
scatter_data = merged[[
    "country", "obs_value",
    "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)",
    "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)"
]].dropna()
scatter_data.columns = ["country", "vax_coverage", "gdp_pc", "life_exp"]

scatter_plot = (
    ggplot(scatter_data, aes(x="gdp_pc", y="vax_coverage"))
    + geom_point(aes(color="life_exp"), size=2.5, alpha=0.7)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="#b30000", se=True)
    + scale_color_gradient(low="#fee8c8", high="#08519c", name="Life Expectancy")
    + scale_x_log10()
    + labs(title="Vaccine Coverage vs GDP per Capita",
           subtitle="Linear regression line shown in red",
           x="GDP per Capita (log scale)", y="Coverage (%)",
           caption="Source: UNICEF + World Bank")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
            plot_background=element_rect(fill="white"))
)
scatter_plot.draw()

Time Series: Global Trend (2018–2022)

Despite disruptions — including the COVID-19 pandemic — global rotavirus vaccine coverage has shown a modest upward trend from 2018 to 2022. However, the pace of progress remains insufficient to meet global health targets.

Code 
ts_plot = (
    ggplot(time_series, aes(x="time_period", y="avg_coverage"))
    + geom_line(color="#b30000", size=1.2)
    + geom_point(color="#b30000", size=3)
    + geom_area(alpha=0.15, fill="#b30000")
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=list(range(2018, 2023)))
    + labs(title="Global Average Rotavirus Vaccine Coverage (2018–2022)",
           x="Year", y="Average Coverage (%)",
           caption="Source: UNICEF")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(plot_title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
            plot_background=element_rect(fill="white"))
)
ts_plot.draw()

Conclusion

The data tells a clear story:

  1. Coverage is unequal — wealthy nations consistently outperform low-income ones
  2. Progress is slow — global averages improved only marginally over five years
  3. GDP and health are linked — economic development strongly correlates with vaccine access

Achieving universal rotavirus vaccination requires targeted investment in health systems, supply chains, and community outreach — especially in the world’s most vulnerable regions.

Data Source: UNICEF Global Database & World Bank Development Indicators
